text.title: Agency-to-Agency Referral Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Agency-to-Agency Referral` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each named partner MUST have a documented niche and ICP that does NOT overlap with the agency's own niche. Overlap > 20% means competitors, not partners.
rule.rationale: Niche-overlap partners compete for the same leads; mapping enforces symmetric value before kick-back terms are written.

rule.statement: Kick-back rate (10-20% of first-engagement value) MUST be written into a partner-agreement document signed by both founders. Handshake terms are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Handshake kick-backs evaporate when the first invoice arrives; written terms make the payout enforceable.

rule.statement: Each partner MUST receive a warm-intro template (subject line, opening, signature) the agency uses for forwarding leads. Cold forwards are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Cold-forwarded leads convert at 5-10% vs warm-intro at 30-50%; template enforces the warm-intro discipline.

rule.statement: Each partner relationship MUST have a quarterly 30-min review with metrics (leads sent, leads received, kick-back paid, kick-back received, problem cases). Drift-without-review is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-reviewed partner relationships go silent at 6 months; quarterly check surfaces imbalance before the partner disengages.

rule.statement: The partner agreement MUST contain a conflict-of-interest clause: when a lead would create a direct competitor conflict for either party, both parties must disclose before accepting.
rule.rationale: Hidden COI conflicts create breach-of-trust events that destroy referral relationships; an explicit clause forces early disclosure.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
